series.name: Inferō: The Decode
series.description: A 53-post deep-dive for ML engineers and researchers covering the full LLM inference stack — from KV caches and speculative decoding, to RLVR and test-time compute, to TPUs and wafer-scale silicon, to attention variants, multimodal inference, diffusion models, and agentic workloads.
series.etymology: From Latin 'inferre' — to carry in, bring forth, draw a conclusion.

naming.chapter_callout_format: Part of *{chapter_title}* — Ch. {N} of Inferō: The Decode

chapters.title: The Machine Room
chapters.subtitle: Production Inference: building systems that serve at scale
chapters.theme: Speed, cost, and throughput at serving time. Every technique fights the memory bandwidth wall from a different angle.

posts.title: Anatomy of Inference
posts.description: Prefill vs. decode, the memory wall, KV cache basics, key metrics: TTFT, TBT, throughput, goodput.
key_papers: Vaswani et al., Attention Is All You Need (NeurIPS 2017)
Kaplan et al., Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models (OpenAI 2020)

posts.title: KV Cache Optimization
posts.description: PagedAttention, prefix caching, KV quantization, eviction strategies, distributed KV (LMCache), prompt compression (LLMLingua).
key_papers: ★ Kwon et al., PagedAttention (SOSP 2023)
Jiang et al., LLMLingua (EMNLP 2023)

posts.title: Attention Head Variants
posts.description: MHA → MQA → GQA → MLA. GQA is the production standard; MLA is DeepSeek's KV compression breakthrough.
posts.note: Moved from Ch. 4 — mainstream production technique.
key_papers: ★ Shazeer, Fast Transformer Decoding: MQA (2019)
★ Ainslie et al., GQA (EMNLP 2023)
DeepSeek-AI, DeepSeek-V2 Technical Report (2024)

posts.title: Sliding Window Attention & Attention Sinks
posts.description: SWA for bounded KV cache; attention sink problem; StreamingLLM fix; production in Mistral 7B, TensorRT-LLM, OpenAI models.
posts.note: Moved from Ch. 4 — shipped in production.
key_papers: ★ Jiang et al., Mistral 7B (2023)
★ Xiao et al., Efficient Streaming LLMs with Attention Sinks (ICLR 2024)

posts.title: Long Context: RoPE Scaling & Position Interpolation
posts.description: Extending context windows to 128K–1M tokens without full retraining. YaRN, NTK-aware RoPE, LongRoPE.
key_papers: Chen et al., Extending Context Window via Positional Interpolation (2023)
Peng et al., YaRN: Efficient Context Window Extension (ICLR 2024)

posts.title: Batching Strategies
posts.description: Static, dynamic, continuous batching (Orca). Chunked prefill to reduce TTFT spikes. 10-50x throughput gains.
key_papers: ★ Yu et al., Orca (OSDI 2022)
Agrawal et al., Sarathi-Serve: Chunked Prefill (OSDI 2024)

posts.title: Speculative Decoding & Multi-Token Prediction
posts.description: Draft-verify (lossless 1.5–3x speedup). Medusa, EAGLE. Multi-Token Prediction training technique (DeepSeek-V3, Meta FAIR).
key_papers: ★ Leviathan et al., Speculative Decoding (ICML 2023)
Cai et al., Medusa (2024)
Li et al., EAGLE (2024)
★ Gloeckle et al., Better & Faster LLMs via MTP (Meta FAIR 2024)

posts.title: Quantization
posts.description: FP16→INT4→FP8 precision ladder. GPTQ, AWQ, LLM.int8(), SmoothQuant. FP8 in H100 and DeepSeek-V3.
key_papers: ★ Dettmers et al., LLM.int8() (NeurIPS 2022)
Frantar et al., GPTQ (ICLR 2023)
Lin et al., AWQ (MLSys 2024)
Xiao et al., SmoothQuant (ICML 2023)

posts.title: Model Parallelism & Disaggregated Inference
posts.description: TP, PP, SP, EP. PD disaggregation: physically separate prefill (H100s) from decode (different chips). DistServe, llm-d, AWS+Cerebras.
key_papers: ★ Shoeybi et al., Megatron-LM (2019/2021)
★ Zhong et al., DistServe: Disaggregating Prefill and Decoding (OSDI 2024)

posts.title: FlashAttention & Kernel Optimizations
posts.description: IO-aware tiling eliminates HBM round-trips. FA1/2/3 progression. FlashInfer. Kernel fusion.
key_papers: ★ Dao et al., FlashAttention (NeurIPS 2022)
Dao, FlashAttention-2 (ICLR 2024)
Shah et al., FlashAttention-3 (NeurIPS 2024)

posts.title: Pruning, Distillation, MoE & Model Routing
posts.description: Structured pruning (Minitron). Knowledge distillation. MoE (Mixtral, DeepSeek-V3, Llama 4). Model routing/cascades (RouteLLM, FrugalGPT).
key_papers: ★ Jiang et al., Mixtral of Experts (2024)
Muralidharan et al., Compact LLMs via Pruning and Distillation / Minitron (NeurIPS 2024)

posts.title: Serving Frameworks
posts.description: vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, SGLang, TGI — comparison on latency, throughput, hardware compatibility.
key_papers: ★ vLLM / Kwon et al. (SOSP 2023)
SGLang / Zheng et al. (MLSys 2024)

chapters.title: The Thinking Layer
chapters.subtitle: RL Inference: making models reason harder, not just faster
chapters.theme: Test-time compute as a new axis of model capability — from simple sampling to pure RL-trained reasoning.

posts.title: Test-Time Compute Scaling
posts.description: The paradigm shift from train bigger to think longer. Inference-time compute vs. pre-training compute tradeoff.
key_papers: ★ Snell et al., Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally (ICLR 2025 Oral)
Wu et al., Inference Scaling Laws (ICLR 2025)

posts.title: Chain-of-Thought and Its Variants
posts.description: Zero-shot CoT, few-shot CoT, self-consistency, Tree of Thoughts, tool-augmented reasoning.
key_papers: ★ Wei et al., Chain-of-Thought Prompting (NeurIPS 2022)
Wang et al., Self-Consistency (ICLR 2023)
Yao et al., Tree of Thoughts (NeurIPS 2023)

posts.title: Reward Models & Verifiers
posts.description: ORM vs PRM. RLVR (verifiable rewards). LLM-as-judge at inference time. Reward hacking.
key_papers: ★ Lightman et al., Let's Verify Step by Step (ICLR 2024)
Cobbe et al., Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems (2021)
Wang et al., Math-Shepherd (ACL 2024)

posts.title: Best-of-N, Voting & Adaptive Compute
posts.description: BoN, majority voting, weighted voting, Certaindex adaptive allocation (Dynasor).
key_papers: ★ Wu et al., Inference Scaling Laws (ICLR 2025)
Fu et al., Dynasor: Certaindex (2024)

posts.title: Tree Search at Inference Time
posts.description: MCTS applied to LLM reasoning. REBASE. When tree search beats sampling.
key_papers: Silver et al., Mastering Go with Deep Neural Networks and Tree Search (Nature 2016)
★ Wu et al., Inference Scaling Laws — REBASE (ICLR 2025)

posts.title: RLHF & PPO
posts.description: The SFT → reward model → PPO pipeline. DPO. The bridge from preference learning to reasoning.
key_papers: ★ Ouyang et al., InstructGPT (NeurIPS 2022)
Schulman et al., Proximal Policy Optimization (2017)
Rafailov et al., Direct Preference Optimization (NeurIPS 2023)

posts.title: GRPO & RLVR: How DeepSeek-R1 Changed Everything
posts.description: RLVR bypasses reward model. GRPO eliminates value model. R1-Zero: AIME 2024 from 15.6% → 71.0%. The aha moment.
key_papers: ★ DeepSeek-AI, DeepSeek-R1 (Nature 2025)
Shao et al., DeepSeekMath / GRPO (2024)

posts.title: Token Efficiency & Overthinking
posts.description: Budget forcing, reasoning depth calibration, REO, fast mode inference.
key_papers: Pfau et al., Let's Think Dot by Dot (COLM 2024)

posts.title: Distilling Reasoning Into Smaller Models
posts.description: SFT on CoT traces. DeepSeek-R1 distillation: 1.5B model outperforms GPT-4o on math.
key_papers: ★ DeepSeek-AI, DeepSeek-R1 distillation section (2025)
Ho et al., LLMs as Reasoning Teachers (ACL 2023)

posts.title: The Unified View
posts.description: Serving reasoning models. KV management at 100K+ token chains. TTRL. Future: online RL inference.

chapters.title: The Foundry
chapters.subtitle: AI Accelerators: the silicon that shapes everything above
chapters.theme: The hardware substrate that determines which inference optimizations are even possible.

posts.title: Why GPUs Dominate (and Their Limits)
posts.description: CUDA lock-in, memory bandwidth bottleneck, NVIDIA roadmap, AMD MI300X, Intel Gaudi 3, NVLink / InfiniBand.

posts.title: Google TPUs
posts.description: Systolic arrays, v7 Ironwood (4,614 TFLOPS/chip), optical circuit switches, JAX/XLA tradeoffs.
key_papers: Jouppi et al., In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a TPU (ISCA 2017)

posts.title: AWS Trainium & Inferentia
posts.description: Trainium2 (2.52 PFLOPS FP8/chip). 500K+ chip Anthropic deployment. AWS+Cerebras disaggregated architecture.

posts.title: Groq LPU
posts.description: Compiled SRAM, 80 TB/s bandwidth, 1,665 tok/s with speculative decoding, deterministic execution.

posts.title: Cerebras WSE
posts.description: 4T transistors, 900K cores, wafer-scale eliminates inter-chip comms, 125 PFLOPS peak.

posts.title: Tenstorrent & Open-Source Silicon
posts.description: RISC-V + Tensix cores, Blackhole (745 TFLOPS FP8, $999), IP licensing, open tt-Metalium toolchain.

posts.title: Edge AI Accelerators
posts.description: Apple ANE (18 TOPS), Qualcomm Hexagon NPU, Hailo-8/10H, on-device LLM requirements.

posts.title: Photonics, Neuromorphic & Next-Gen Architectures
posts.description: Lightmatter (photonic), D-Matrix (in-memory compute), Intel Loihi (neuromorphic), optical interconnects in TPU pods.

chapters.title: The Attention Wars
chapters.subtitle: Attention Variants: the mechanism is no longer settled
chapters.theme: As of 2026, every major lab has made a different bet on attention architecture.

posts.title: The Quadratic Problem & the Design Space
posts.description: Why softmax attention can't scale infinitely. The full spectrum: Softmax → Sparse → Linear → SSM → Hybrid. Where each frontier model sits.

posts.title: Linear Attention & Gated DeltaNet
posts.description: Katharopoulos 2020 → RetNet → GLA → DeltaNet → Gated DeltaNet (ICLR 2025). 3:1 hybrid in Qwen3.5 and Kimi Linear. MiniMax rollback — the honest counterpoint.
key_papers: ★ Katharopoulos et al., Transformers are RNNs (ICML 2020)
★ Yang et al., Gated Delta Networks: Improving Mamba2 with Delta Rule (ICLR 2025)

posts.title: Mamba & SSM Hybrids
posts.description: Selective state spaces, Mamba-2, 1:7 hybrid ratio. Jamba 1.5, Nemotron-H (3x throughput), Bamba-9B, FalconMamba in production.
key_papers: ★ Gu & Dao, Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling (2023)
Dao & Gu, Transformers are SSMs / Mamba-2 (ICML 2024)
★ Lieber et al., Jamba (ICLR 2025)

posts.title: Sparse Attention & The Honest Verdict
posts.description: DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA). GLM-5: MLA + DSA. The verdict matrix: what's production-ready vs. still a bet.

chapters.title: Beyond the Word
chapters.subtitle: Multimodal & Generative Inference: when models see, hear, and generate
chapters.theme: What changes when models process images, video, and generated media — and the unique inference challenges each modality brings.

posts.title: How Multimodal LLMs Work
posts.description: Late fusion vs. early fusion. Token count problem: 336×336 = 576 tokens; 1080p tiled = 2,000–5,000 tokens.

posts.title: The Visual Token Bottleneck
posts.description: Spatial/semantic redundancy. KV cache explosion. Inverse scaling: fewer tokens + larger LLM wins.

posts.title: Visual Token Compression
posts.description: Q-Former, Resampler, TokenPacker. FastV (54% FLOP reduction), SparseVLM, ATP-LLaVA, Matryoshka Multimodal Models.

posts.title: High-Resolution & Tiling
posts.description: Dynamic tiling (LLaVA-HD). Qwen2.5-VL Window-Attention ViT. VL-CACHE modality-aware KV eviction. MiniCPM-V on mobile.

posts.title: Video Inference
posts.description: Temporal redundancy. LLaMA-VID (2 tokens/frame). PruneVid. STORM (NVIDIA) long video.

posts.title: Vision Encoders
posts.description: CLIP vs. SigLIP vs. DINOv2 vs. native tokenization (GPT-4o). Qwen2.5-VL Window-Attention ViT.

posts.title: Multimodal KV Cache
posts.description: Modality asymmetry. VL-CACHE eviction. Shared prefix caching for repeated images. Inf-MLLM streaming.

posts.title: How Diffusion Models Work
posts.description: Denoising loop. Latent diffusion (VAE). CFG doubling cost. UNet → DiT backbone (SD3, FLUX, Sora).
key_papers: Ho et al., DDPM (NeurIPS 2020)
Rombach et al., Latent Diffusion Models (CVPR 2022)
★ Peebles & Xie, DiT: Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers (ICCV 2023)

posts.title: Accelerating Diffusion Inference
posts.description: DDIM (50x step reduction). DPM-Solver (10-20 steps). Consistency models (1-4 steps). SDXL-Turbo, FLUX.1-schnell.
key_papers: ★ Song et al., DDIM (ICLR 2021)
Lu et al., DPM-Solver (NeurIPS 2022)
★ Song et al., Consistency Models (ICML 2023)

posts.title: Video Diffusion & the Temporal Challenge
posts.description: Temporal attention for frame consistency. DiT at video scale (Sora). Efficient video generation techniques.
key_papers: Ho et al., Video Diffusion Models (NeurIPS 2022)
OpenAI, Sora: Video generation models as world simulators (2024)

posts.title: Frontier Multimodal Architectures
posts.description: GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Llama 4, Qwen2.5-VL, InternVL2, DeepSeek-VL2. Unified tokenization vs. vision tower.

chapters.title: The Long Game
chapters.subtitle: Agentic Inference: when a single query becomes a multi-hour task
chapters.theme: Agentic inference breaks every single-turn assumption. It's about completing tasks that take hours, not serving responses in milliseconds.

posts.title: The Anatomy of an Agentic Workload
posts.description: The ReAct loop in production. SWE-bench: 50–200 LLM calls, 200K+ tokens per 2-hour trajectory. Task completion rate per dollar as the key metric.

posts.title: The Context Explosion Problem
posts.description: Context grows across turns. Lost in the middle at 100K+ tokens. Truncation, summarization, RAG for agent memory, LLMLingua compression.

posts.title: KV Cache for Agents: Persistence, Reuse & TTL
posts.description: KV cache spans hours across tool call idle time. Continuum KV TTL scheduling. KVFlow for multi-agent workflows. Isolation requirement.
key_papers: Continuum: Efficient Multi-Turn LLM Agent Scheduling with KV Cache TTL (arXiv 2025)
Agentic Plan Caching (OpenReview 2025)
KVFlow for multi-agent workflows (NeurIPS 2025)

posts.title: Tool Call Latency: The Bottleneck Nobody Optimizes
posts.description: Tool execution = 60–80% of wall-clock time. Parallel tool calls (4x speedup). Async execution. Critical path analysis.

posts.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
posts.description: When to spawn sub-agents. Critical execution path determines end-to-end latency. DAG orchestration. Agent routing. 8 small-model runs often beats 1 large-model run.

posts.title: Agentic Memory
posts.description: In-context vs. external vs. parametric memory. EM-LLM episodic memory. MemOS: agent memory as OS with tiers.
key_papers: MemOS: A Memory OS for AI Systems (arXiv 2025)
EM-LLM: Human-Inspired Episodic Memory for Infinite Context LLMs (ICLR 2025)

posts.title: Cost Management at Scale
posts.description: 200-step trajectory × $0.01/call × 1K users = $2K/day on one workflow. Certaindex adaptive allocation. Budget-constrained orchestration.
key_papers: Fu et al., Dynasor: Certaindex for Adaptive Reasoning Compute (2024)
Damani et al., Learning How Hard to Think (2024)

posts.title: Serving Infrastructure for Agents
posts.description: Session-aware routing (87% cache hit rate with llm-d). State isolation. Fault tolerance for 2-hour trajectories. MCP protocol. Kubernetes + Argo at 8K parallel runs.
